roadmap.rationale: Pure-drop of `commit_index` from Phase 1 single-mode per ADR-0020. The field has no consumer, is structurally redundant with `spec_digest`, cannot meet its Phase 2 forward-compat promise, and has generated three production bugs in 26 hours. Single-cut migration discipline applies — Phase 1 is unshipped. The DELIVER PR replaces the per-write surface with `spec_digest` + `IdempotencyOutcome`, narrows `ClusterStatus` to four fields, and reverts the snapshot frame from v2 to v1. The complete surgery surface — every file with line numbers, change shape, and rationale tag — is enumerated in `docs/feature/redesign-drop-commit-index/design/upstream-changes.md`.

validation.rationale: Reviewed by Atlas (nw-solution-architect-reviewer) on 2026-04-26. Verdict: APPROVED with zero blocking issues. All 3 walking-skeleton scenarios (WS-1/WS-2/WS-3 per test-scenarios.md Amendment 2026-04-26 ADR-0020) covered. Surgery surface (upstream-changes.md §1-§12) completely decomposed across 5 steps in correct order. Architectural decisions locked (snapshot frame v1, IdempotencyOutcome location in api.rs, ClusterStatus pure-drop rationale). Quality gates comprehensive and ordered from weakest to strongest. Single-cut migration discipline preserved.

phases.name: Drop commit_index across trait, impl, handlers, API, CLI, tests, and docs
phases.description: Single-cut migration per project memory `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations`. Phase 1 is unshipped; no external consumer; no deprecation path. The five steps below partition the surgery surface enumerated in `upstream-changes.md` along verification-gate boundaries: each step has a distinct CI signal that proves it landed correctly. Step 01-01 lands the wire-contract change coherently (trait + handlers + api.rs cannot ship piecemeal without compile breakage). Step 01-02 lands the storage-layer simplification under the new contract. Step 01-03 regenerates the OpenAPI schema and updates CLI rendering — both are downstream of the wire change and gate on `cargo xtask openapi-check` + CLI render acceptance tests. Step 01-04 deletes and revises the test surface that asserted on the dropped field. Step 01-05 amends the architectural records and runs the §12 catch-all grep audit. Each step is a single commit; the PR lands as one breaking-internal change.

steps.name: Land the wire contract — trait surface, handlers, api.rs
steps.description: Drop `commit_index` from the `IntentStore` trait surface (return type of `get`, fields on `PutOutcome::Inserted` and `PutOutcome::KeyExists`, all related docstrings). Introduce the `IdempotencyOutcome` enum colocated with the other API response types in `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/api.rs`. Update the three handlers (`submit_job`, `describe_job`, `cluster_status`) to consume the new trait surface and emit the new response shapes. This step lands the wire contract change coherently — splitting trait, api.rs, and handlers across multiple steps would require placeholder shims that violate single-cut migration. The crate compiles after this step against the new shapes; the impl crate (`overdrive-store-local`) compiles because its `LocalIntentStore` already carries the data the new variants need (the `commit_index` field becomes dead code, deleted in 01-02). Implements §1 (trait surface) + §3 (handlers.rs and api.rs) of `upstream-changes.md`.
criteria: `crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/intent_store.rs` — `IntentStore::get` returns `Result<Option<Bytes>, IntentStoreError>` (drop the `u64`); `PutOutcome::Inserted` is a unit variant; `PutOutcome::KeyExists` carries only `existing: Bytes`; all docstrings on `PutOutcome`, `get`, and `watch` revised to drop per-entry / store-wide / inline-framing / Phase-2-stable-signature framing.

`crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/api.rs` — `SubmitJobResponse` is `{job_id, spec_digest, outcome}`; `JobDescription` is `{spec, spec_digest}`; `ClusterStatus` is `{mode, region, reconcilers, broker}`; new `IdempotencyOutcome` enum with derives `Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize, ToSchema` and `#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]`, variants `Inserted` | `Unchanged`.

`crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/handlers.rs` — `submit_job` matches `PutOutcome` to compute `IdempotencyOutcome`, computes `spec_digest` from the rkyv archive, returns the new shape; the byte-equality check inside `KeyExists { existing }` decides between `Unchanged` (200) and 409 Conflict; `describe_job` no longer destructures `(Bytes, u64)`; `cluster_status` no longer reads `LocalStore::commit_index()`.

`cargo nextest run -p overdrive-core -p overdrive-control-plane --lib` is GREEN (unit-level only — integration tests asserting on `commit_index` are revised in 01-04 and may still fail on this step; intentionally-RED test surface is acceptable per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § 'RED scaffolds and intentionally-failing commits' — commit with `--no-verify` if pre-commit gates reject and call it out in the commit body).

`cargo check --workspace` is GREEN — no compile errors anywhere in the workspace; the `overdrive-store-local` impl carries the data the new variants need without changes (the `commit_counter` field becomes dead code, deleted in 01-02).

Commit-message shape: `feat!(intent-store): drop commit_index from trait + API surface (ADR-0020)`. Breaking is internal-only — Phase 1 is unshipped — but the `!` flags it for changelog tools. Body cites ADR-0020 and `Step-ID: 01-01`.
steps.implementation_notes: Compile-ordering: the `overdrive-store-local` impl carries `commit_counter: AtomicU64` and a public `commit_index()` accessor on `LocalIntentStore`. After this step the trait no longer surfaces those, but the impl can still own the field as dead code without compile error — the dead code becomes deletable in 01-02. The handler change in `submit_job` requires recomputing `spec_digest` on the conflict path to compare against `existing`; this computation already exists for `JobDescription` (per ADR-0020 §Decision §2) and is reused. `IdempotencyOutcome::Unchanged` is returned when the byte-equality check passes on `KeyExists { existing }`; 409 Conflict is returned when bytes differ — the `outcome` field never carries `"conflict"` (conflict is HTTP-status, not enum value, per ADR-0015 §4 amendment). The `IdempotencyOutcome` enum location and derives are decision-locked in `upstream-changes.md` §3 — do not re-open. Per `.claude/rules/development.md` § Newtype completeness, `IdempotencyOutcome` is small enough that `FromStr`/`Display` are not required; serde-via-rename_all is the canonical wire form.

steps.name: Simplify single-mode impl + revert snapshot frame v2 → v1
steps.description: Delete the `commit_counter`, `peek_next_inside`, `bump_commit_after_commit`, public `commit_index()` accessor, inline `[u64-LE-prefix || value]` row encoding, and all three `debug_assert_eq!` calls (lines 312, 392, 525 — re-grep at edit time per `upstream-changes.md` §2). Simplify `put`, `put_if_absent`, `delete`, `txn`, and `bootstrap_from` to the shapes that do not include the bump/peek dance. Revert `snapshot_frame.rs` from v2 to v1 — encoder emits `Vec<(Vec<u8>, Vec<u8>)>` under schema discriminator v1; the v1 decoder is preserved verbatim; v2 frame definition / encoder / decoder branch deleted entirely; `pub const VERSION: u16 = 1;` (or the `_V1` alias is unified to a single constant — crafter picks the cleaner mechanical edit). Apply downstream-of-trait edits across the sim crate (grep `crates/overdrive-sim/` for `commit_index`, `(Bytes, u64)`, `PutOutcome::Inserted { commit_index }`, `PutOutcome::KeyExists { commit_index, .. }` — each match becomes the same mechanical edit as the production-side equivalent; there is no standalone sim adapter file for IntentStore — the harness reuses the real LocalIntentStore on a per-host tempdir per `upstream-changes.md` §2). Implements §2 (single-mode impl + snapshot frame + sim downstream) of `upstream-changes.md`.
criteria: `crates/overdrive-store-local/src/redb_backend.rs` — no `commit_counter` field; no `peek_next_inside` / `bump_commit_after_commit` helpers; no public `commit_index()` accessor; no `encode_entry` / `decode_entry` inline-framing helpers; zero `debug_assert_eq!` calls (verify by grep — was 3 at lines 312/392/525); `put` / `put_if_absent` / `delete` / `txn` / `bootstrap_from` all simplified per `upstream-changes.md` §2; module docstring rewritten end-to-end (Per-entry / inline framing / Phase 2 forward-compat sections deleted).

`crates/overdrive-store-local/src/snapshot_frame.rs` — `pub const VERSION: u16 = 1;` (single constant); encoder emits `Vec<(Vec<u8>, Vec<u8>)>`; v2 encoder/decoder branch deleted; v1 decoder branch preserved verbatim; module docstring revised to drop the 'v1 inputs project the missing index column to 0' framing (the v1 frame is canonical).

`crates/overdrive-sim/` — grep-clean for `commit_index`, `(Bytes, u64)`, `Option<(Bytes, u64)>`, `PutOutcome::Inserted { commit_index`, `PutOutcome::KeyExists { commit_index`. Each prior match has been edited to the trait's new shape (drop tuple unpack, drop field bind, drop index assertion).
`cargo nextest run -p overdrive-store-local -p overdrive-sim --lib` is GREEN.

`cargo xtask dst` is GREEN — the harness invariant `intent_store_returns_caller_bytes` (per ADR-0020 §Enforcement) is added if not yet present, asserting that `IntentStore::get` returns the bytes as supplied to `put` with no framing overhead. This is the structural-regression guard against a future contributor re-introducing inline encoding.
Commit-message shape: `refactor(store-local): drop commit_counter and revert snapshot frame to v1 (ADR-0020)`. Body cites ADR-0020 §Decision §3 + §Enforcement and `Step-ID: 01-02`.
steps.implementation_notes: On-disk format change: redb files written by the current code are not readable by the post-step code (rows are no longer prefixed with the `[u64-LE-prefix || value]` frame). This is deliberately accepted — Phase 1 development databases are scratch, no production data exists to migrate. The cross-writer race documented in `f5b361c`'s RCA is unreachable post-step because the bump call site is gone. The `bootstrap_from` simplification: the previous code read a `commit_index` from the snapshot frame and seeded `commit_counter` via `fetch_max`; the new code clears the `entries` table and replays snapshot rows verbatim. The sim crate audit: the file paths to grep are under `crates/overdrive-sim/src/` — `harness.rs` is the most likely site (per `upstream-changes.md` §2 it reuses `LocalIntentStore`), but the audit covers the whole crate. Snapshot proptests live in `tests/integration/snapshot_proptest.rs` and are revised in 01-04 — the impl-level edits here may make them fail until 01-04 lands; that is intentionally-RED per `.claude/rules/testing.md`. The `intent_store_returns_caller_bytes` invariant is the structural-regression guard required by ADR-0020 §Enforcement; if it does not yet exist, the `files_to_modify` above includes its creation site (`crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/intent_store_caller_bytes.rs` — verify the actual `invariants/` module path at edit time).
quality_gates: cargo nextest run -p overdrive-store-local -p overdrive-sim --lib passes
cargo xtask dst passes (with intent_store_returns_caller_bytes invariant)
grep clean for commit_index across overdrive-sim

steps.name: Regenerate OpenAPI schema + update CLI rendering
steps.description: Regenerate `api/openapi.yaml` from the new Rust types via `cargo xtask openapi`, then verify `cargo xtask openapi-check` passes. The expected diff: `SubmitJobResponse` drops `commit_index`, adds `spec_digest` (string) and `outcome` (enum); `JobDescription` drops `commit_index`; `ClusterStatus` drops `commit_index`; new `IdempotencyOutcome` component schema (string enum `inserted` | `unchanged`). Update CLI rendering across `render.rs` (three `Commit index:` lines at 32, 81, 100 — re-grep at edit time), `cli.rs` (line 91 rustdoc), `commands/cluster.rs` (`ClusterStatusOutput` field set), `commands/job.rs` (submit + describe rendering), `commands/alloc.rs` (drop any 'Commit index' line). The `job submit` output prints the outcome in human form (`created` for `inserted`, `unchanged` for `unchanged`) — the JSON wire form is `inserted` | `unchanged`, the CLI surfaces the human form per `upstream-changes.md` §5. The walking-skeleton test-scenarios amendment (test-scenarios.md §1.1, §1.2, §1.3) maps directly to this step's CLI rendering output. Implements §4 (OpenAPI regen) + §5 (CLI rendering) of `upstream-changes.md`.
criteria: `api/openapi.yaml` regenerated via `cargo xtask openapi`; `cargo xtask openapi-check` is GREEN. Three component schemas changed (`SubmitJobResponse`, `JobDescription`, `ClusterStatus`); one new component schema (`IdempotencyOutcome`).

`crates/overdrive-cli/src/render.rs` — three `Commit index:` writeln sites deleted (was lines 32, 81, 100); `render::job_submit` prints `Spec digest:` + `Outcome:` (human form `created` | `unchanged`); `render::alloc_status` no longer prints a Commit-index line; `render::cluster_status` output narrows from five lines to four (`Mode`, `Region`, `Reconcilers`, `Broker`).
`crates/overdrive-cli/src/cli.rs` — line 91 rustdoc updated to drop the `commit_index` mention.
`crates/overdrive-cli/src/commands/cluster.rs` — `ClusterStatusOutput` (or equivalent) drops `commit_index: u64`; print logic mirrors the four-field shape.
`crates/overdrive-cli/src/commands/job.rs` — submit handler prints `spec_digest` + `outcome`; describe handler prints `spec` + `spec_digest`.
`crates/overdrive-cli/src/commands/alloc.rs` — no `Commit index` line in alloc-status rendering.

`cargo nextest run -p overdrive-cli --lib` is GREEN (CLI render acceptance tests live in `tests/acceptance/render_*.rs` and are revised in 01-04 — they may still fail on this step intentionally; the lib-level CLI tests still gate this step).
Commit-message shape: `refactor(cli,api): regenerate OpenAPI + update CLI rendering for ADR-0020 wire shape`. Body cites ADR-0020 §Decision §2/§4 and `Step-ID: 01-03`.
steps.implementation_notes: Do NOT hand-edit `api/openapi.yaml` — it is generated. Run `cargo xtask openapi` and check in the generated bytes; `cargo xtask openapi-check` then asserts no drift between generator output and checked-in YAML. Per memory `feedback_no_user_install_instructions`, do not surface install hints if `cargo xtask openapi` is missing — just run it. The label set printed by `render::job_submit` (per `upstream-changes.md` §7 `render_job_submit.rs` enumeration line 12) is: `Accepted.`, `Job ID:`, `Intent key:`, `Spec digest:`, `Outcome:`, `Endpoint:`, `Next:`. The walking-skeleton scenarios in `distill/test-scenarios.md` §1.1, §1.2, §1.3 (Amendment 2026-04-26 ADR-0020) describe the operator-visible behaviour that the CLI rendering must produce — those scenarios are translated into Rust acceptance tests in `crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/walking_skeleton.rs` and revised in 01-04. CLI render fixture revisions (`render_job_submit.rs`, `render_alloc_status.rs`, `render_cluster_and_node.rs`) are also revised in 01-04 — this step lands the source-side rendering changes; 01-04 lands the matching test-side fixture revisions.

steps.name: Test surface — delete obsolete tests, revise survivors
steps.description: Delete the four test files that exist solely to assert properties of `commit_index` (`tests/integration/commit_counter_invariant.rs`, `tests/acceptance/per_entry_commit_index.rs`, `tests/integration/per_entry_commit_index.rs`, `tests/acceptance/commit_index_monotonic.rs` — verify exact paths via `crates/{store-local,control-plane}/tests/` greps at edit time). Revise the test surface that asserts on adjacent properties: 11 enumerated test files across the store-local crate, the control-plane crate, and the CLI crate. Each revision shape is enumerated explicitly in `upstream-changes.md` §7 with line citations from the 2026-04-26 grep audit. The walking-skeleton CLI integration test (`crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/walking_skeleton.rs`) is revised per the test-scenarios.md Amendment 2026-04-26 (ADR-0020) block — WS-1 asserts on `outcome == "inserted"` + digest equality (lines 120-122); WS-2 asserts on `broker.dispatched > 0` plus the `reconcilers` registry list and the absence of a Commit-index line (lines 135-137); WS-3 asserts on `outcome == "unchanged"` plus digest equality across re-submits. The mod-declaration files (`tests/integration.rs`, `tests/acceptance.rs`) are updated to drop the `mod` lines for deleted files. Implements §6 (delete-in-full: 4 test files) + §7 (revise: 14 test files including all CLI integration tests + control-plane tests) of `upstream-changes.md`.
criteria: Deleted in full: `crates/overdrive-store-local/tests/integration/commit_counter_invariant.rs`, `crates/overdrive-store-local/tests/acceptance/per_entry_commit_index.rs`, `crates/overdrive-store-local/tests/integration/per_entry_commit_index.rs`, `crates/overdrive-store-local/tests/acceptance/commit_index_monotonic.rs` (verify exact paths at edit time — the `crates/...` prefix is the operative path).

Mod-declaration files updated: any `mod commit_counter_invariant;` / `mod per_entry_commit_index;` / `mod commit_index_monotonic;` lines removed from `tests/integration.rs` / `tests/acceptance.rs` entrypoints.

Revised — store-local acceptance: `phantom_writes.rs` (drop counter-bump assertion, preserve no-emit assertion; rename if appropriate), `snapshot_roundtrip.rs` (assert v1 frame round-trips key+value, no per-entry index column), `put_if_absent.rs` (assert variant only, drop index value assertion), `local_store_basic_ops.rs` (drop tuple unpack), `local_store_edges.rs` (drop tuple unpack).
Revised — store-local integration: `snapshot_proptest.rs` (generator emits `Vec<(Vec<u8>, Vec<u8>)>`, no index column).

Revised — control-plane integration: `concurrent_submit_toctou.rs` (drop any commit_index assertion; TOCTOU property preserved by `outcome == unchanged` + back-door byte-equality), `idempotent_resubmit.rs` (assert `outcome == unchanged` + spec_digest equality across N re-submits; rename test to drop `commit_index` reference), `submit_round_trip.rs` (assert `outcome == inserted` + spec_digest equality, drop `>= 1` assertion), `describe_round_trip.rs` (assert spec_digest equality between submit and describe responses, drop commit_index assertion), `submit_job_idempotency.rs` (assert outcome + digest, drop commit_index equality).

Revised — control-plane acceptance: `runtime_registers_noop_heartbeat.rs` line 202 (drop `assert_eq!(body.commit_index, state.store.commit_index(), ...)`; assert on four-field ClusterStatus + broker.dispatched advancement instead), `api_type_shapes.rs` lines 43/48/55/62/71/80 (drop `commit_index` field from three fixtures + three round-trip assertions; replace SubmitJobResponse round-trip with spec_digest + outcome assertions, JobDescription round-trip with spec_digest only, ClusterStatus round-trip with four-field shape).

Revised — CLI integration: `http_client.rs` lines 141/146 (drop `commit_index > 0` and `description.commit_index == submit_resp.commit_index`; replace with `outcome == Inserted` + digest equality), `job_submit.rs` lines 10/15/129-131 (rustdoc + assertion), `endpoint_from_config.rs` lines 94-96 (assertion), `cluster_and_node_commands.rs` line 12 (rustdoc-only), `walking_skeleton.rs` lines 120-122/135-137 (WS-1 outcome + digest; WS-2 broker.dispatched).

Revised — CLI acceptance render fixtures: `render_job_submit.rs` lines 12/28/43/59 (rustdoc + fixture + label list + assertion message), `render_alloc_status.rs` lines 12/20/32/110/143 (rustdoc + four fixtures), `render_cluster_and_node.rs` lines 12/28/44/62 (rustdoc + fixture + label list `["Mode:", "Region:", "Reconcilers:", "Broker counters:"]` + assertion).
`cargo nextest run --workspace` is GREEN — every assertion now matches the post-ADR-0020 wire shape.
`cargo test --doc --workspace` is GREEN — any rustdoc example referencing `commit_index` has been updated.
Commit-message shape: `test: drop commit_index test surface and revise survivors for ADR-0020`. Body cites ADR-0020 and `Step-ID: 01-04`.
steps.implementation_notes: Verify each test file's exact path via grep at edit time — the upstream-changes.md document cites paths as `tests/integration/<file>.rs` style; the actual on-disk paths are scoped to a specific crate (e.g. `crates/overdrive-store-local/tests/integration/commit_counter_invariant.rs`). Per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § Layout, integration-shaped tests live under `tests/integration/` and acceptance-shaped under `tests/acceptance/`; do not let the upstream-changes.md `tests/integration/...` shorthand confuse the actual crate-scoped path. The walking-skeleton revisions here MUST match the test-scenarios.md §1.1 / §1.2 / §1.3 amendment block (Amendment 2026-04-26 ADR-0020) — that is the spec; the test is the implementation. The §12 catch-all grep audit lives in 01-05; this step's gate is that `cargo nextest run --workspace` is GREEN. Some renamed test functions (e.g. `byte_identical_resubmit_returns_outcome_unchanged_and_same_digest`) are noted in `upstream-changes.md` §7 — apply the rename in this step. The `phantom_writes.rs` test name change (e.g. `phantom_writes_dont_emit_events`) is also applied here. Per memory `feedback_no_rename_stubs`, do not leave behind rename-marker stubs of deleted tests — the four deleted files are deleted with their `mod` declarations; no compatibility stubs.
deliverables: 14 test files revised across store-local + control-plane + CLI
walking-skeleton WS-1/WS-2/WS-3 aligned with test-scenarios.md amendment

steps.name: Architecture amendments + brief edits + grep-audit completion
steps.description: Amend ADR-0008 (REST/OpenAPI transport) and ADR-0015 (HTTP error mapping) with `> **Amendment 2026-04-26 (ADR-0020).**` blocks at the head of each file (matching the project's existing amendment-block style). ADR-0008's endpoint table is revised to list the three responses with the new field sets; ADR-0015 §4 idempotent-success row is revised from `200 OK, same commit_index as the original` to `200 OK, same spec_digest as the original, with outcome: IdempotencyOutcome::Unchanged`. ADR-0020's status flips from Proposed to Accepted (project ADR convention — Status flips when the change-set lands on `main`). Edit `docs/product/architecture/brief.md` to revise the three references inline (no strikethrough — the brief is architecture SSOT, not an amendable artifact; references are deleted/replaced cleanly per `upstream-changes.md` §9). Run the §12 catch-all grep audit: `grep -rln 'commit_index\|Commit index' crates/ tests/ api/ | grep -v target/ | grep -v evolution/` returns zero matches. Run mutation testing per the per-feature strategy. Implements §8 (ADR amendments) + §9 (brief edits) + §12 (verification gates) of `upstream-changes.md`. Per project memory `feedback_delegate_to_architect`, ADR amendments and brief edits are architect-owned — the crafter executing this step works through the architect agent for these specific files, not inline.
criteria: `docs/product/architecture/adr-0008-rest-openapi-transport.md` — Amendment 2026-04-26 (ADR-0020) block at file head; endpoint table revised: `POST /v1/jobs` returns `{job_id, spec_digest, outcome}`, `GET /v1/jobs/{id}` returns `{spec, spec_digest}`, `GET /v1/cluster/info` returns `{mode, region, reconcilers, broker}`; cites ADR-0020 as source.

`docs/product/architecture/adr-0015-http-error-mapping.md` — Amendment 2026-04-26 (ADR-0020) block at file head; §4 status-code matrix idempotent-success row revised to `200 OK, same spec_digest as the original, with outcome: IdempotencyOutcome::Unchanged`; 409 Conflict row unaffected; cites ADR-0020 as source.

`docs/product/architecture/adr-0020-drop-commit-index-phase-1.md` — Status flipped from `Proposed. 2026-04-26.` to `Accepted. 2026-04-26.` (per project ADR convention — Status flips when the change-set lands on `main`).

`docs/product/architecture/brief.md` — three `commit_index` references revised inline (no strikethrough): per-entry idempotency-contract references replaced with `spec_digest` + `IdempotencyOutcome`; cluster-status references removed (no replacement field).
ADRs 0013 and 0014 are NOT amended (verified by grep at amend time — both have zero `commit_index` references per ADR-0020's grep audit; this is documented as intentional non-amendment).

Catch-all grep audit: `grep -rln 'commit_index\|Commit index' crates/ tests/ api/ | grep -v target/` returns zero matches. Any docs/ surface that retains `commit_index` is correctly preserved as historical record (the `docs/evolution/` files capturing the bug cascade, the `docs/feature/.../deliver/roadmap.json` files for prior features) — verify each remaining match is justified.
`cargo nextest run --workspace` is GREEN (every step's gate must still hold at the end).
`cargo test --doc --workspace` is GREEN.
`cargo xtask openapi-check` is GREEN.
`cargo xtask dst` is GREEN.

`cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main` returns kill-rate ≥ 80% per the per-feature mutation strategy. Run with `run_in_background: true` per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § 'Mutation testing is the exception'.
Commit-message shape: `docs(architecture): amend ADRs 0008/0015 + brief + flip ADR-0020 to Accepted`. Body cites ADR-0020 and `Step-ID: 01-05`.
steps.implementation_notes: Per project memory `feedback_delegate_to_architect`, ADR amendments and brief.md edits go through the architect agent (Morgan), not inline editing by the crafter. The crafter dispatches to architect for these four files; the crafter runs the grep audit, the test gates, the OpenAPI check, the DST gate, and the mutation gate directly. The grep audit is the definitive completeness check per ADR-0020 §Enforcement and `upstream-changes.md` §12 — zero matches outside `docs/` is the bar. Each remaining `docs/` match is verified justified: `docs/evolution/2026-04-25-fix-commit-index-per-entry.md`, `docs/evolution/2026-04-25-fix-commit-counter-and-watch-doc.md`, `docs/evolution/2026-04-26-fix-commit-counter-and-watch-doc.md` (bug-cascade historical record), `docs/feature/phase-1-control-plane-core/deliver/roadmap.json` and `docs/feature/fix-commit-index-per-entry/deliver/roadmap.json` (frozen feature roadmaps), `docs/feature/redesign-drop-commit-index/...` (this feature's own DESIGN/DELIVER artefacts including this roadmap.json — the references are by-design). Per memory `feedback_fix_clippy_dont_defer`, any clippy issues surfaced during the workspace-wide checks land in this step; do not defer to follow-up. Per memory `feedback_no_unrequested_ci_lefthook`, do not auto-wire any new tests into CI/lefthook beyond what already gates the workspace. Mutation gate semantics: per-PR, diff-scoped via `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main`; kill-rate ≥ 80% per the per-feature strategy in this repo. After the mutation run completes (pass, fail, or cancel), run `git checkout -- crates/` to restore any mutated source the wrapper did not clean up itself per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § 'Mutation testing is the exception'.
quality_gates: grep audit zero matches outside docs/

review.findings_summary: 10 findings, all resolved (DISTILL coverage, surgery surface coverage, step ordering, sizing flags non-blocking per project convention, single-cut discipline, quality gates, locked decisions, ADR ownership).
praise: 37-file surgery surface partitioned across 5 steps with each step having exactly one observable outcome
All four upstream-changes.md peer-review findings (snapshot frame, IdempotencyOutcome location, test enumeration, sim adapter path) incorporated with locked decisions and explicit citations
Implementation notes carry load-bearing detail; crafter has no ambiguous decision points
Quality gates ordered weakest to strongest (unit tests → workspace nextest → mutation testing); respects CI budget and feedback velocity
